07-REQ-001.note: Library consumed by the Snek Centaur Server runtime — one player per hosted team per live game, sharing the process's access to the team's Centaur state and its game's SpacetimeDB instance. Coordinator/worker-pool topology is mechanism (design.md).

07-REQ-002.note: Depth-1 (single-ply) lookahead is binding MVP scope: each scored world is exactly the next turn's resolution; no multi-ply tree.

07-REQ-003.note: Per-owned-snake stateMap (direction -> worst-case weighted score) continuously updated during the turn in response to the reactive inputs.

07-REQ-005.note: Never writes authoritative game state; staged moves for automatic-mode snakes are the sole SpacetimeDB write channel. The authority-placement principle behind it is global-invariants' (gi flag from the matrix checked — no re-authoring); what retires here is the framework-side write-channel behaviour.

07-REQ-006.note: The two author abstractions: Drive (snake- or cell-targeted directed motivation) and Preference (targetless scalar function).

07-REQ-007.note: The enumerated Drive operations (reward, distance, motivation combiner, satisfaction predicate, target-eligibility predicate, self-direction nomination, foreign-move nomination) authored at intent grain in the requirement body. Purity categories and invocation sites are mechanism (design.md).

07-REQ-008.note: Preference = targetless, time-invariant [-1,1] function of self snake and board; no distance or satisfaction concept.

07-REQ-009.note: All heuristic scalars in [-1,1] (distance non-negative and finite); relative importance exclusively via portfolio weights.

07-REQ-009a.note: Strict boundary enforcement of the range invariants: validate every author output, clamp out-of-range, substitute safe defaults for non-finite/wrong-typed values; nothing invalid ever propagates downstream. The contained-author-exception behaviour (Design-mined lead) is authored alongside.

07-REQ-009b.note: Structured violation reporting to the hosting server's process log, deduplicated per turn per (snake, heuristic, violation kind), dedup resetting on turn change; not persisted and not surfaced in the operator UI (resolved 07-REVIEW-014 posture carried at intent grain — the exact log-field list is mechanism).

07-REQ-010.note: Terminal reward on satisfaction bypasses distance/motivation; a Drive satisfied on the turn's observed authoritative board is retired at turn close (retirement anchor per 07-REVIEW-007).

07-REQ-011.note: Goal/Fear distinction is author-level semantics only; runtime treats them identically.

07-REQ-012.note: No algebraic assumptions about author operations beyond the range constraints.

07-REQ-013.note: Portfolio definition: active Preferences with weights, active Drives with concrete targets and weights, one effective softmax temperature. Per the author-resolved cycle-break, temperature is authored as an opaque already-derived scalar; its derivation (07-REQ-056 cluster) is bot-configuration's.

07-REQ-019.note: Candidate self-direction enumeration; immediately lethal directions deprioritised but retained as last resort, so an alive snake always has at least one candidate.

07-REQ-020.note: Exactly three reactive inputs: interest map (union of active Drives' foreign-move nominations), per-foreign-snake commitment, portfolio weights.

07-REQ-021.note: SPLIT per the author decision: the activation predicate (a world is active iff each assumed foreign move is in the interest map and consistent with commitment) is authored; the no-re-simulation half (toggling never re-simulates) is mechanism — code, rationale in this change's design.md (mechanism-boundary section).

07-REQ-023.note: Cache cleared only when the observed turn number changes; same-turn reconnection never clears (07-REVIEW-008); within a turn evaluation work only grows. The append-only cache data structure itself is mechanism.

07-REQ-032.note: SPLIT: the frozen-in-place semantics for out-of-interest foreign snakes retire onto foreign-snake-treatment; the timestamp carriage onto frozen-snake-timestamps. The wrapper-composition realisation (engine untouched) is mechanism — design.md; the one-shared-engine invariant it protects is global-invariants/one-shared-engine.

07-REQ-033.note: Foreign snake means any snake other than the one being evaluated — alive teammates included.

07-REQ-034.note: Commitment semantics per category: manual teammate's effective staged move commits only when it intersects the evaluator's interest map (07-REVIEW-002); automatic teammates and opponents always uncommitted — a framework-staged move never commits its own teammate.

07-REQ-035.note: stateMap entry = worst-case weighted score over currently active worlds; undefined until at least one active world is evaluated for the direction.

07-REQ-036.note: Weighted score = sum of weight x (motivation, or terminal reward where satisfied, or Preference value); min aggregation lives in worst-case-statemap; dirty flag set on any actual stateMap entry change.

07-REQ-038.note: SPLIT across two requirements of the same capability: higher-entries-are-better polarity in worst-case-statemap; softmax-favours-higher in softmax-decision. Conservative-minimax naming is design vocabulary, not spec.

07-REQ-040.note: Three attention tiers (automatic; selected manual; unselected manual) authored at intent grain with the no-starvation guarantee; within-tier round-robin detail is mechanism with 07-REQ-041. Included per the assignment matrix's bot-framework row (seam: operator selection drives tier 2).

07-REQ-048.note: Softmax sampling proportional to exp(entry / effective temperature) for automatic-mode snakes; temperature consumed as the portfolio's opaque scalar; the randomness source is deliberately unconstrained (mechanism).

07-REQ-049.note: Undefined entries excluded from the distribution; fallback to lastDirection, or stage nothing on the first turn so the engine's random fallback resolves the move (game-engine/movement).

07-REQ-051.note: Selection of a manual-mode snake promotes it to the selected tier effective immediately; deselection demotes — driven by operator-control/exclusive-selection state.

07-REQ-052.note: Promotion re-evaluates active/dormant status against current reactive inputs and rescores the stateMap; the resulting snapshot write is the decision-transparency story's (triggered by the dirty flag authored here).

07-REQ-053.note: Selection promotion never stages a move — display and attention only, consistent with operator-control/selection-is-view-only.

07-REQ-054.note: An operator-staged move present at promotion survives untouched.

07-REQ-057.note: All persistent state in the Centaur state subsystem; game tree caches, stateMaps, and dirty flags are in-memory per-game scratch. The matrix's gi flag checked — the runtime-ownership principle is already global-invariants'; what retires here is the framework's own statefulness boundary.

07-REQ-058.note: Subscribes to the team's game-scoped Centaur state for real-time reactivity — operator edits propagate without polling.

07-REQ-059.note: Never calls Centaur state mutations on operators' behalf; operator mutations flow client-to-Convex directly and the framework observes their effects.

07-REQ-060.note: Board, staged moves, turn number, and chess clock read from the SpacetimeDB subscription; game-instance state never cached in Convex.

07-REQ-065.note: Every simulated board carries per-snake turn timestamps: advanced snakes at the simulated turn, frozen snakes at the prior turn. The concrete field shape is mechanism; the readable-staleness behaviour is the spec.

07-REQ-066.note: Board-analysis consumers (framework or heuristic authors) compensate for the frozen-in-place fiction via a temporal head start proportional to staleness (07-REVIEW-003); the multi-source-BFS seeding pattern is illustrative, not mandated.

07-REQ-024.note: Mechanism (author-resolved boundary): the append-only simulated-world cache via the shared engine. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md (mechanism-boundary section). Observable residue authored as bot-framework/turn-scoped-evaluation and worst-case-statemap#one-turn-horizon.

07-REQ-025.note: Mechanism: cached worlds store weight-independent normalised heuristic outputs. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md. Observable residue: score-composition#zero-weight-silences.

07-REQ-026.note: Mechanism: portfolio weights applied at scoring time, never folded into the cache. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md. The live-editability guarantee it serves is the bot-configuration story's.

07-REQ-027.note: Mechanism: the lattice structure of foreign-move combinations. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md.

07-REQ-028.note: Mechanism: per-(foreign snake, direction) priority weights with rank decay. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md (plastic performance choice — doc-comment with a design reference).

07-REQ-029.note: Mechanism: anytime traversal of uncomputed active lattice points by descending combined priority. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md. Observable residue: softmax-decision#partial-statemap-is-decidable.

07-REQ-030.note: Mechanism: Dijkstra-like axis-neighbour traversal without revisits. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md.

07-REQ-031.note: Mechanism: priority changes reorder only still-uncomputed points; computed worlds never re-simulated. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md.

07-REQ-041.note: Mechanism: within-snake round-robin across candidate directions (breadth-first on rank 0). Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md. The tier-level ordering it schedules under is authored as bot-framework/attention-tiers.

07-REQ-042.note: Mechanism: reactive rescoring from the existing cache without new simulation. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md. Observable residue: score-composition dirty-flag-on-change and reactive-inputs#dormant-worlds-reactivate.

07-REQ-043.note: Mechanism: newly active uncomputed points enqueued in the snake's tier; reverted commitments reactivate without enqueueing. Stays in code; rationale in this change's design.md.

07-REVIEW-002.note: A manual teammate's staged move outside the evaluator's interest map adds no explored alternative — the teammate is frozen in place, consistent with the standard lattice-membership rules.

07-REVIEW-003.note: Temporal head-start compensation: consumers of simulated boards seed frozen snakes ahead proportional to their staleness, compensating for the frozen-in-place fiction.

07-REVIEW-006.note: Sampling over a partial stateMap is legitimate anytime behaviour: undefined entries are excluded, and a decision from the evaluated subset beats no decision.

07-REVIEW-007.note: Drive retirement is anchored to the turn's observed authoritative board only — satisfaction in simulated worlds contributes terminal reward but never retires.

07-REVIEW-008.note: A SpacetimeDB reconnection that resurfaces the current turn number keeps the evaluation cache; only an observed turn-number change clears it.
